---
title: "<span style='font-size: 30px'>RCVS Victimization Survey Variable Change Codebook(18-21-24)</style>"
author:
- affiliation: Director IRL EUSPb
  name: Ruslan Kuchakov
- affiliation: Researcher IRL EUSPb
  name: Dmitrii Serebrennikov
- affiliation: Researcher IRL EUSPb
  name: Dmitry Bobrikov
- affiliation: Assistant Professor in the Department of Criminology at the University of South Florida
  name: Alex Knorre
- affiliation: Associate Professor of Empirical Legal Studies at European University at Saint Petersburg
  name: Dmitry Skougarevskiy
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output:
  html_document:
    encoding: UTF-8
---

![](img/ipp_logo_eng.png){#id .class width="274" height="110px"}
![](img/eusp_logo_eng.png){.class width="274" height="px"}

The codebook provides information on how variables have changed in three waves of the RCVS in 2018, 2021, and 2024. The symbols in the table below have the following meanings:

*N* — variable is not present in the wave.

*Y* — variable is present in the wave.

*Q* — variable is present in the wave, but the question has been modified.

*O* — variable is present in the wave, but the answer options in the question have been modified.

*F* — variable is present in the wave, but the filter for it has been modified.

*(variable name)* — variable is present in the wave, but is named differently.

The presence of multiple letters from the list Q, O, F indicates that there were multiple changes.

```{r setup, echo=F, warning=FALSE}
library(kableExtra, quietly = T)
library(data.table)
options(knitr.kable.NA = ' ')

qof_changes <- fread("../../data/supplementary_data/qof_changes.csv")
qof_changes[, names(qof_changes) := lapply(.SD, iconv, from = "CP1251", to = "UTF-8")]
names(qof_changes) <- iconv(names(qof_changes), from = "CP1251", to = "UTF-8")

qof_changes[, `Значение переменной` := NULL]

names(qof_changes) <- c("ID", "Variable label", "2018", "2021", "2024")

kable(qof_changes, align = "l") %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 10, full_width = F) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = T)
```
